text: Attention mechanisms have proven highly effective in enhancing feature representations for various computer vision tasks by modeling spatial or channel dependencies. However, many existing methods rely on channel dimensionality reduction to manage computational costs, which can inadvertently obscure deep visual semantics. In this paper, we introduce the Efficient Multi-Scale Attention (EMA) module, a novel framework designed to improve representational power while maintaining computational efficiency. The EMA module avoids dimensionality reduction by reshaping a portion of the channels into the batch dimension and partitioning channel dimensions into multiple sub-group features, ensuring that spatial semantic information is well-distributed within each group. To capture comprehensive dependencies, we employ large-scale parallel subnetworks that encode global information for channel re-calibration. These parallel branches are then integrated through a cross-spatial learning method, utilizing cross-dimension interaction to capture critical pixel-level pairwise relationships. Rigorous evaluation across multiple benchmarks—including CIFAR-100, ImageNet-1k, MS COCO, and VisDrone2019—demonstrates that EMA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art attention mechanisms in image classification and object detection tasks while requiring fewer parameters and lower computational overhead.
